element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Character building system allows pre-generation or random generation with player modification
Three-dimensional first-person perspective with viewport display; players learn spatial navigation around Phlan
Combat follows AD&D encounter tables; players learn positioning and turn-based tactical system

element_introduction_order.element: Class-specific abilities (fighter attacks, magic-user spells, thief backstab)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Different classes unlock strategic options; fighters gain multi-attack and sweep abilities at higher levels
Menu-driven conversation system; alignment affects NPC interactions
Item and currency management between party members

element_introduction_order.element: Spell casting and magic skill acquisition
element_introduction_order.first_appears: For magic-user and cleric classes from start
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Menu-driven spell selection and learning system

element_introduction_order.first_appears: First combat with threat of character HP reaching zero
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Requires another character to bandage fallen allies or face permanent death
Culmination of adventure; confronting main antagonist in Phlan surroundings

typical_tension_triggers: Character HP dropping below critical threshold
Presence of powerful monsters from Monster Manual
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based tactical rhythm with immediate action resolution; random encounters interrupt exploration flow; combat intensity governed by enemy difficulty and character positioning

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory combat encounters following standard AD&D encounter tables; difficulty scales with character level progression
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increasing monster variety and encounter complexity; players master class-specific tactics and positioning strategies
difficulty_curve.late_game: Culmination in boss encounter with powerful enemy leader; requires strategic use of all learned abilities and party coordination
notable_spikes: First major combat encounter requiring tactical positioning

notable_level_patterns.description: Random encounters structured using standard AD&D Monster Manual encounter tables; enemy depictions replicate tabletop game experience
First-person exploration in safe modes interrupted by random encounters; viewport display shows party status while text provides context
Different character classes access different combat options: fighters use melee/ranged weapons with multi-attack progression, magic-users cast spells, thieves backstab via positioning
Players can view character movement from different angles including aerial perspective; dimetric projection in combat mode supports spatial decision-making
